title: Dry Crumbly Argentinian Beef Empanadas
description: A traditional Argentinian dish featuring beef and herbal flavors, perfect for a post-workout snack, with vegetarian-friendly options by substituting beef with portobello mushrooms or eggplant, and these empanadas can be made crumbly by using a mixture of all-purpose and corn flour, and the beef stock concentrate adds a rich flavor to the filling
instructions: In a large bowl, combine all-purpose flour, corn flour, and salt.
Gradually add olive oil to the flour mixture, stirring with a fork until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Add beef stock concentrate, rest-meat or portobello mushrooms, onion, hard-boiled egg, black pepper, and dried oregano to the flour mixture, stirring until just combined.
Turn the mixture out onto a lightly floured surface and knead a few times until it comes together.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a tablespoon of the filling in the center of each circle and fold the dough in half, pressing the edges together to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little water and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
